scenarios.opening_query: The birth injury changed our lives forever and the hospital's own review admitted 'process failures.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || General Litigation > Wrongful Death
scenarios.hidden_fact: Our daughter survived two months in the NICU and passed in March — the case is her death, brought by us as her parents.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She survived two months in the NICU and passed in March — the case is her death, brought by us.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She survived and is thriving in some ways — but the hypoxic injury means lifelong care, which is what the case must fund.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She survived — but the injury means lifelong care, and the case has to fund that.
scenarios.vetting_note: Hardest disclosure in the set; the label difference is real and consequential.

scenarios.opening_query: Our architect's design failed inspection twice and the redesign fees are on us somehow.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > General Litigation || Real Property > Construction/Contractors || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The builder built AHEAD of the corrected drawings and now both point at each other — the construction-defect tangle with the GC is the bigger case than the architect alone.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The builder built ahead of the corrected drawings and now they blame each other — the construction-defect tangle is the bigger case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether builder defects intertwine with the design error
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's been built — it's purely the architect's professional error and his fee demand.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing's built yet — it's purely the architect's error and his fee demand.
scenarios.vetting_note: Built-vs-unbuilt fact merges or separates the defendants.

scenarios.opening_query: A tax preparer chain botched three years of my returns and the penalties landed on me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > General Litigation || Consumer Law > General
scenarios.hidden_fact: Their ads promise 'penalty protection' and 'accuracy guarantee' they refuse to honor — the deceptive-guarantee practice is the consumer case, on top of the sloppy work.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Their ads promise 'penalty protection' they won't honor — the deceptive guarantee is the case, beyond the sloppy work.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether advertised guarantees create a consumer-deception claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No guarantees involved — an independent CPA just made errors, and professional negligence is the claim.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No guarantees — an independent CPA just made errors.

scenarios.opening_query: The counselor our court ordered for our son shared his file with people who had no right to it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Health Law/HIPAA || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's a county juvenile-department employee — the disclosure went to his school through official channels, so it's a claim against the county with the notice deadline.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's a county juvenile-department employee — it went to his school through official channels, so it's a county claim with the notice deadline.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the discloser is a public employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She's in private practice on the court's referral list — a private-provider confidentiality breach.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's private practice off the referral list — a private confidentiality breach.
scenarios.vetting_note: Public-employee overlay on a privacy breach.

scenarios.opening_query: My realtor 'forgot' to submit our counteroffer and we lost the house we'd been chasing for a year.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > General Litigation || Administrative Law > Professional Licensing
scenarios.hidden_fact: We learned she 'forgot' because the winning buyer was her own brother-in-law — the real-estate board complaint for the license violation is the lever we want pulled first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She 'forgot' because the winning buyer was her brother-in-law — we want the board complaint pulled first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a licensing-board complaint is the chosen lever
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No conflict — just carelessness; our damages suit for the lost bargain is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No conflict — just careless. The damages suit is the case.
scenarios.vetting_note: Self-dealing fact opens the regulatory route users often prefer.

scenarios.opening_query: The nursing home let my father wander out an unlocked door in January. He was found hours later.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || Wills & Trusts > Long-term Care Issues
scenarios.hidden_fact: He recovered, thank God — but the facility now demands we hire private 1:1 aides or accept discharge; fighting the discharge and care-planning terms is the immediate need.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He recovered — but now they demand private aides or discharge. Fighting that discharge and the care plan is the immediate need.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an injury claim or care-planning/discharge fight is the need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He lost three fingers to frostbite — the negligence case for his injury is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He lost three fingers to frostbite — it's the negligence case.
scenarios.vetting_note: Outcome-severity fact separates injury litigation from care advocacy.
